title: Nationalism and Revolutions
description: This exercise will focus on how revolutions developed around the world between 1750 and 1900 CE, using the theme of GOV. It will have 2 types based on the examples of revolutions and the development of nationalisms, scaled x6 (total 12)
topic_path: Nationalism and Revolutions in the Period from c. 1750–1900 CE | 5.2

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Haitian Revolution (1791–1808) occurred in the wealthiest colony in the world, upending the French plantation system and redistributing the land to formerly enslaved people. Which of the following factors most directly contributed to the success of this revolution?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the unstable social structure in the colony
an alliance with the United States
eventual independence from the French
the superior military might of the revolutionaries

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**The event shown in the image is best understood in the context of which of the following broader processes in Europe from 1700 to 1800 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The painting shows a room full of delegates and some around a desk.
options.caption: Source: "Declaration of Independence" by John Trumball (1819), depicting the five-man drafting committee of the Declaration of Independence presenting their work to the Congress in 1776. Public domain.

choices.content: Wars meant higher taxes in colonies.
State expansion meant turmoil in the colonies.
Monarchs were losing power and influence.
Social divisions meant unrest in the colonies.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments in France best illustrates how the French Revolution was influenced by the American Revolution? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the establishment of a representative democracy
the Third Estate declaring itself the National Assembly
the declaration of a constitutional monarchy
the end of feudalism and aristocratic privileges

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following revolutionary movements are most closely modeled on previous Atlantic revolutions between 1750 and 1900? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Haitian Revolution
The American Revolution
The Pueblo Revolt
The Taiping Rebellion

assessment_items.question_content:
**The ruling classes of West Africa encountered several revolutionary movements between 1600 and 1900 CE. How might a historian argue these movements were similar to other Atlantic revolutions? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They were driven by an ideology of reform and liberation.
They were based on Enlightenment ideals of equality and freedom.
They were motivated by Catholic ideas of spiritual equality.
They were about overthrowing a colonial power.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Taiping Rebellion (1851–1864) occurred during a time of economic turmoil under the Qing Dynasty in China. Which of the following were promises made by the revolutionary leader, Hong Xiuquan, to gain the support of the peasants? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Limits on foreign trade and to improve the standard of living
Provide a form of self-government through a constitutional monarchy
Move the capital to Nanjing and reform the government
Replace Confucianism with ideals of equality shown in Christianity and Buddhism

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.
**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the letter above and your knowledge of World History, which of the following best represents the basis of national identity after the revolution in Haiti?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: An image of a handwritten letter in untranslated French.
options.caption: Source: Letter in French to a French minister from Toussaint Louverture (1801). Inspired by the French Revolution, Louverture wrote this letter during his last year as governor of Haiti to the French Minister of Marine and the Colonies, appealing to universal liberty and freedom. Public domain.

choices.content: Enlightenment ideals
a shared language
ethnic identity
a common religion

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best illustrates how former British colonies formed a national identity after the American Revolution? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Ideals of equality and self-government in a democracy created a sense of purpose.
Diversity of religion, language, and ethnic heritage created a sense of pride.
Unity of language created a sense that American English was the best form of English.
Wealth of natural resources created a sense of pride in an agricultural economy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**During the French Revolutionary period, war and expansion influenced the development of a French national identity. Which of the following best represents how the French defined this shared identity? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a history of winning and a clear definition of what they were not
political ideals and acceptance of diverse cultural backgrounds
economic prosperity and belief in representative democracy
strong military leadership and the shared desire to expand their territory

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows:
**

>It is a grandiose idea to think of consolidating the New World into a single nation, united by pacts into a single bond. It is reasoned that, as these parts have a common origin, language, customs, and religion, they ought to have a single government to permit the newly formed states to unite in a confederation. But this is not possible. Actually, climatic differences, geographic diversity, conflicting interests, and dissimilar characteristics separate America.

-From “Reply of a South American to a Gentleman of This Island [Jamaica]” by Simone Bolivar.

**The author of the excerpt above would likely agree with which of the following statements about the development of nationalism in Latin America between 1750 and 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: Selected Writings of Bolivar, edited by Harold A. Bierck, Jr., translated by Lewis Bertrand, 103-122. New York: The Colonial Press, Inc., 1951. https://library.brown.edu/create/modernlatinamerica/chapters/chapter-2-the-colonial-foundations/primary-documents-with-accompanying-discussion-questions/document-2-simon-bolivar-letter-from-jamaica-september-6-1815/

choices.content: There is disunity despite shared ethnicity, language, and culture.
There is unity around shared ethnicity, language, and culture.
There is unity around the shared belief in Enlightenment ideals.
There is disagreement about the government structure of new states.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best illustrates the development of ethnic nationalism between 1750 and 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Greek independence in 1830 CE
American independence in 1776 CE
Haitian independence in 1804 CE
Mexican independence in 1821 CE

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best illustrates the effect of growing ethnic nationalism on the Habsburg Empire and the Ottoman Empire between 1750 and 1900?  
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The formation of new nations
The territorial expansion into central Europe
The development of new forms of government
The imposition of one language and religion
